title: PoC Demonstration
subtitle: Energy Company — Agentic EA in Action
description: Proof of concept demonstrating agentic Enterprise Architecture with synthetic but realistic Energy Company data.

hero.headline: From Theory to Practice
hero.subline: A working proof of concept with Energy Company's architecture: Neo4j graph database, 32 MCP tools, and a Business Architect Agent producing real deliverables from a living repository.

context.title: Energy Company
context.description: Global power and energy technology company headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland. Major business areas include Grid Automation, Grid Integration, High Voltage Products, Transformers, and Service & Operations.
highlights: Critical infrastructure — grid operations are life-safety systems
Complex application landscape (Lumada APM, SCADA/EMS, SAP S/4HANA, IoT platforms)
Hybrid technology stack (on-premise SCADA + Azure cloud + edge computing)
Strong AI adoption trajectory (predictive maintenance, grid optimization, digital twins)

deliverable_samples.title: Predictive Maintenance AI Agent Coverage
deliverable_samples.input: Analyze capability gaps for implementing predictive maintenance using AI agents across all grid assets
deliverable_samples.summary: Analysis revealed 3 of 8 capabilities have partial AI coverage. Predictive Maintenance capability has the highest readiness (score 4/5) with Lumada APM and the Predictive Maintenance Agent already in place. Asset Management and Grid Protection have significant gaps — no AI agents assigned, limited data integration.
key_findings: Predictive Maintenance: partial coverage — agent exists but lacks grid-wide deployment
Asset Management: missing — no AI agent, manual health scoring
Grid Protection: missing — safety-critical, requires human-in-the-loop design
Grid Monitoring: fully covered — SCADA + Grid Optimization Agent + real-time telemetry

deliverable_samples.title: Legacy SCADA Replacement Impact
deliverable_samples.input: Assess the impact of replacing legacy SCADA/EMS with a cloud-native grid management platform
deliverable_samples.summary: Replacing the SCADA/EMS system (he-app-comp-002) impacts 87 elements across 5 layers. 12 direct dependencies include Grid Telemetry Store, OPC-UA Gateway, and 3 AI agents. Safety-critical: Grid Protection and Fault Diagnosis systems depend on SCADA real-time data feeds.
key_findings: 87 elements impacted across Business, Application, Technology, Physical, AI layers
12 direct dependencies, 34 indirect, 41 potential
[SAFETY-CRITICAL] Grid Protection relies on SCADA telemetry — migration requires parallel-run
3 AI agents (Grid Optimization, Fault Diagnosis, Predictive Maintenance) need reintegration
Air-gapped SCADA network must be redesigned for cloud connectivity

deliverable_samples.title: Transformer Order-to-Delivery Agent Readiness
deliverable_samples.input: Assess the Transformer Order-to-Delivery process for AI agent readiness
deliverable_samples.summary: Overall readiness: 2.8/5. Design sub-process scores highest (3.5) with PLM integration and Design Validation Agent. Assembly scores lowest (1.5) — manual, safety-critical, limited data capture. Testing is ready for AI augmentation (score 3.0) with Digital Twin potential.
key_findings: Design: 3.5/5 — PLM system + Design Optimization Model available
Procurement: 2.5/5 — SAP integration exists but manual supplier qualification
Assembly: 1.5/5 — manual, safety-critical, human-in-the-loop required
Testing: 3.0/5 — Digital Twin platform could enable AI-assisted test validation
Shipping: 3.0/5 — Supply Chain Portal provides tracking, agent could optimize logistics

deliverable_samples.title: Agent Orchestration Protocol Selection
deliverable_samples.input: Select agent orchestration protocol for grid operations
deliverable_samples.summary: Evaluated MCP, A2A, and custom protocol. Decision: MCP as primary with A2A for inter-agent communication. MCP aligns with 4 of 6 architecture principles (Interoperability Standards, Secure by Design). Custom protocol rejected — violates Interoperability Standards principle.
key_findings: MCP selected: mature tooling, stdio transport, broad LLM support
A2A complementary: agent-to-agent workflows for multi-step grid operations
Custom protocol rejected: maintenance burden, violates Interoperability Standards
Safety principle satisfied: both MCP and A2A support human-in-the-loop patterns

deliverable_samples.title: Grid Automation Technology Landscape
deliverable_samples.input: Generate Grid Automation technology landscape view for CTO review
deliverable_samples.summary: Layered view scoped to Grid Automation showing 52 elements across 4 layers. Technology layer: 3 Azure K8s clusters, SCADA servers, edge nodes. Application layer: SCADA/EMS, Lumada APM, IoT Platform, 3 AI agents. Business layer: Grid Monitoring, Fault Management, Predictive Maintenance processes.
key_findings: 52 elements in view, spanning Technology → Application → Business → Strategy
Hybrid infrastructure: on-premise SCADA + Azure cloud + edge computing at substations
3 AI agents active in Grid Automation scope
Single point of failure: SCADA/EMS serves 8 downstream components

components.name: Neo4j Community Edition
components.detail: Docker-based, property graph with native Cypher traversal

components.name: Python MCP Server
components.detail: 32 tools via Model Context Protocol (stdio transport)

components.name: Claude Code
components.detail: Business Architect persona with CLAUDE.md instructions

components.name: ArchiMate 3.2
components.detail: 60 element types + 8 AI stereotypes, ~300 relationship rules

components.name: Pydantic
components.detail: Input/output schema validation for all 32 tools
